generic_rules: Use the lightest governance sufficient for the milestone risk, while preserving explicit project authority and approval gates.
Treat dependency edges, shared-file ownership, approval gates, integration, and final acceptance as serial barriers unless the project explicitly proves a safer boundary.
Run focused verification before managed full verification, and reuse evidence while its bound inputs remain unchanged.
Use worktree isolation only when project authority names the mode, base, ownership, submit authority, and cleanup policy.
Keep corrective work inside its authorized paths and invariant; require targeted re-review when authority, security, validation, or evidence integrity is affected.
Use one primary review topology and batch findings; open severity or authority findings block milestone acceptance.
Completion requires project acceptance criteria, required evidence, gate disposition, and a bounded record of remaining gaps.
